source_citation: [REAL, cited — see build_reference_data.py] Real cited range 27-33 km/day, Indian-cities urban mobility study; midpoint used.

[REAL, cited but LOWER CONFIDENCE — see build_reference_data.py] Derived from a 2003 three-cities vehicle-characteristics study (urbanemissions.info) reporting a comparison city's car annual mileage as '10,400 km, only 15% lower than Delhi' -> Delhi ~= 10,400/0.85 ~= 12,235 km/yr ~= 33.5 km/day. Could not verify against the primary PDF (no PDF-rendering tool in this environment) — taken from a search-engine-surfaced summary of the study, not the source document directly. Distinct from and less certain than the BS6 limits above.

[REAL, cited — see build_reference_data.py] No e-rickshaw-specific citation found; reused the two-wheeler figure. Note: e-rickshaws have a 0 g/km emission factor, so this distance value doesn't affect the computed load either way.
